Every claim it resolves builds the coverage-decision graph, the proprietary dataset of real payer behavior that powers our next product, Surely, which prices the true cost of a health plan for employers and brokers.

name: Verdant
one_liner: AI-native land-use permit management for local gov
long_description:
Verdant is building the AI infrastructure for local government, and we’re starting with AI-native end-to-end permitting software that dramatically cuts local government permit review time. 

We help applicants file complete, compliant applications upfront, then help staff review them - our AI reads documents, plans, and diagrams, then flags issues and drafts the final report. Staff just sign off.

Pandemic-era incumbent contracts are churning because staff are stuck with legacy systems that only intake and track permits – no automation involved. Our end-to-end solution picks up where those systems stop, automating the review itself to fix permitting where it's most broken.

name: GUILD
one_liner: AI-Native Defense Contractor Reshaping the Defense Supply Chain
long_description:
GUILD is an AI-native defense contractor that automates the Defense Supply chain for the DoW. We predict government demand, then manufacture in-house cheaper and faster than the incumbents.

We've created an AI-native business model for contracting operations, manufacturing, and finance to deliver on defense contracts end-to-end.

name: RealPact
one_liner: AI-native OS for Real Estate Brokerages
long_description:
RealPact builds AI agents that handle the paperwork behind real estate transactions. They find property records like deeds, tax records, permits, parcel data, and MLS information, then use that data to fill out contracts, organize documents, track deadlines, and help brokerages close deals faster.

We are starting by automating transactions and will expand into other parts of brokerage operations. Much of the software used across the real estate industry is outdated. With today’s advances in AI, we can build agents that handle this operational work and create modern software around how brokerages actually work.

We currently sell to brokerages in New Hampshire and are expanding quickly.
subindustry: Real Estate and Construction -> Housing and Real Estate

name: Dialogus
one_liner: Infra for enterprise voice agents.
long_description:
Dialogus builds voice agent infrastructure for enterprises.

Most companies can demo an AI voice agent, but getting one into production is hard. Enterprise agents need to connect to phone systems, internal tools, databases, CRMs, messaging channels, human escalation flows, monitoring, and compliance processes.

Dialogus provides the infrastructure to deploy production-grade voice agents that don’t just talk but complete real workflows. Our platform lets enterprises launch agents that collect information, validate it against internal systems, update records, send follow-up messages, and escalate edge cases to humans.

name: Truffle
one_liner: The autonomous back-of-house for restaurants.
long_description: Truffle is the first AI-native operating system for the hospitality back-of-house. Restaurants, cafes and bars still run on a patchwork of disconnected point solutions, spreadsheets, clipboards and tribal knowledge, which costs the industry billions each year in stockouts, food waste, and lost profit. Truffle replaces that entire stack with one system. Computer vision and voice AI capture real-time inventory levels 90% faster than manual counts, ML models forecast item-level demand at 97% accuracy, and AI agents run dozens of end-to-end workflows autonomously, from procurement and invoice reconciliation to scheduling, food prep, menu engineering, and more.

name: Glen
one_liner: Unified organizational context for agents and humans
long_description:
Glen is unified context for every agent and human on your team. Most MCP tools hand an agent a slice, one Slack thread, a single diff, and leave it to stitch the rest together itself. Glen reads from where your work already lives, code and PRs, issues, docs, and meetings, reconciles it, and gives your agents the full picture instead.

With Glen a coding agent knows why a line was written, not just what it says. Ask a PR assistant anything about a change and the clarifying-question relay disappears. Bug bots read the intent behind the code before they comment, so they stop flagging deliberate choices as defects and stop repeating mistakes they already made. A sales agent can coach your head of sales off the full account history, how teammates handle similar calls, what was promised, where the deal stalled, etc. 

As the work gets done, Glen also distills it into skills and offers them back to the next agent, or person, who hits the same task.

name: Context.dev
one_liner: We give AI agents realtime web context.
long_description:
Context.dev gives AI agents structured data from any website through a single API.

We help developers build smarter agents & products that depend on accurate, fresh web data.

If you need structured data from the internet, you need Context.dev

Proud to power agentic products at Mintlify, Super, Vizzy, Klarna, and 280 other companies.

name: Codag
one_liner: Systems log compression for agents.

name: Touchmark
one_liner: Solving AI pricing

name: Bylaw
one_liner: Guarantee your agents use the right evidence before they act.
long_description:
Bylaw is a check that runs before an AI agent writes to your systems. If the agent is about to act on evidence that is wrong, outdated, or don’t agree across sources, we block the action or send it to a human first. 

We all met our first year at the University of Waterloo studying computer science. We have previously interned at Meta, Optiver, and BitGo, places where making decisions on unverified data could have lethal reprecussions. We kept running into the same failure deploying agents: the agent was allowed to call the tool, but nobody had verified the facts behind the call.

Guardrails ask “can this agent use this tool?” Evals catch mistakes after the fact. Neither checks whether chat, docs, CRM, and tool results actually agree at the moment of the write. That’s why the same refund or CRM mistake keeps coming back even after you fix the prompt.

This is hard because evidence lives in five places at once, goes stale between lookup and action, and every write needs different proof. It hasn’t been productized because agent teams ship fast and patch with prompts; platforms build one-off checks instead of a reusable gate in front of every sensitive tool call.

The same way finance built approval gates before money moves, every company giving agents write access will need this layer.

name: Cerenovus
one_liner: Aggregate company knowledge and make inferences
long_description:
Cerenovus is a company brain. We aggregate every kind of file a company produces — documents, PDFs, emails, Slack messages, spreadsheets, meeting notes — and convert them into a single markdown knowledge graph with native AI-agent integration. A company's information becomes uniformly readable both by humans and by the AI agents working inside the graph.

On top of that foundation, Cerenovus maps the company as a system, drawing connections between the people, processes, and tools that make up real workflows. From that systems map, it infers where operations are inefficient and how to improve them.

In practice, this tool will help executives make better decisions. Whenever a leader has to act on a question — how to restructure a team, whether to keep a vendor, or where handoffs between teams are breaking down — Cerenovus will give them an evidence-backed answer in minutes rather than weeks.

Today, that work is done by consultants, by internal analysts, or by the executive asking around informally. Each of those approaches is slow, partial, and goes stale the moment it is delivered. Cerenovus produces the same answers faster, at a fraction of the cost, and keeps them live as the company evolves.

name: Molagri
one_liner: Biopesticides that are safe for humans and bees.
long_description:
Molagri develops safer, more effective pesticides for agriculture.

We design biopesticides that hit a precise molecular target unique to pest insects, knocking out pests while sparing pollinators, beneficial insects, and the wider ecosystem. By modeling how pests are likely to evolve, we stay a step ahead of resistance, keeping our products effective against the pests of today and tomorrow. The higher efficacy and biobased production will also contribute to emissions reductions in both manufacturing and field use.

Our founders are PhD lab mates from the University of Toronto who have worked together for six years and published in Nature Communications and Nature Microbiology.

name: FlowManual
one_liner: AI for Construction's Back Office.
long_description:
FlowManual is the all-in-one AI platform for construction's back office.

Built by two engineers and Harvard dropouts, FlowManual streamlines workflow by cutting down on manual and repetitive tasks, allowing contractors to win more work and deliver at a higher standard.

name: Talentpluto
one_liner: Voice agents that represent people's careers
long_description:
Resumes turned people into documents. Online profiles turned people into searchable pages. AI creates the next shift. As agents become the primary way companies search and evaluate talent, people will need agents that understand their career context, make them discoverable, and represent them professionally.

Talentpluto is building that agent. Pluto has real voice conversations with people about their work, goals, strengths, and preferences, then turns that understanding into a living professional representation.

For companies, Pluto automates the recruiting work of sourcing, screening, qualifying, and selling candidates. Hiring teams get warm introductions to candidates who have already been vetted, matched to the role, and confirmed as interested.

More than 15,000 people have completed conversations with Pluto, and we are growing 30% month over month. Talentpluto has worked with 100+ companies, from fast-growing startups to unicorns and decacorns.

name: machine0
one_liner: Persistent & powerful cloud VMs from your CLI
long_description:
machine0 is a CLI that lets you create, provision, and snapshot cloud VMs. `machine0 new my-vm` spins up a VM with dedicated CPU, RAM, and optional GPUs, plus a static IP and HTTPS endpoint. The tools agents need (e.g. Node, Python, Docker) and the agents themselves (e.g. Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Hermes) come pre-installed. 99.99% VM uptime SLA across four regions.

Agent workloads are moving from ephemeral to always-on. A coding agent debugging a complex issue runs 4-8 hours. OpenClaw runs 24/7. machine0 is the environment in which these agents run.

name: Bloomy
one_liner: AI-powered mastery learning for K-12
long_description: Bloomy helps students learn more efficiently by using AI to identify exactly what a student should learn and when they should learn it. We provide adaptive and personalized curriculum to K-12, starting with English Language Arts, Math, and Writing.

name: 6thSense
one_liner: A nervous system for physical AI

name: Nori
one_liner: Sub-1500$ robot anyone can program
long_description: We manufacture humanoid robots in San Francisco.

name: Greypoint Industries
one_liner: Manufacturing the future of autonomous swarm warfare.
long_description: Greypoint Industries builds mass-produced autonomous interceptors that defeat drone saturation for a fraction of the cost of the targets. We are developing a soldier-deployable, fire-and-forget interceptor built to give Western and allied forces the ammunition to counter mass drone swarm attacks.

name: Whitespace
one_liner: AI Operating System for Wholesale Distributors
long_description: Whitespace is the AI operating system for wholesale distributors. It connects to their systems and inboxes to build a living model of their business, then deploys self-improving AI agents to automate work like inventory planning, customer support, and order entry.

name: Risklytics
one_liner: AI-powered catastrophe risk modeling
long_description: We build AI-powered catastrophe risk models that help insurers, reinsurers, and financial institutions better understand and quantify natural disaster risk. Our platform combines continuously updated data, property-level analysis, and synthetic catastrophe simulations to deliver more accurate, transparent, and flexible risk assessments than traditional catastrophe models.

name: LATO
one_liner: Agent-native research and simulation platform for investors
long_description:
The best investments come from conviction, and conviction comes from understanding a market deeply: its customers, its competitors, and where it's heading.

Today that understanding is slow and expensive to build. Reaching the right people takes weeks, a commercial study costs $300k+, and each one starts from scratch.

LATO does this research end-to-end, combining public data and the fund's own knowledge with first-hand interviews of a market's customers, experts, and operators. This research grounds a simulation of the market to predict how market participants behave.

name: Isengard Industries Inc
one_liner: Mass-produced AI strike systems and Counter-UAS
long_description:
Isengard Industries is not another drone company.

We build sovereign production: the means for allied nations to make precision-strike and counter-drone systems by the thousand, on their own soil, at a fraction of what the primes charge.

We call it Weapons of Mass Production. The Arsenal of Democracy, rebuilt for the drone age.

More to come, and only where it helps. You know how to reach us.

name: Denta
one_liner: The dental practice that runs itself
long_description:
Denta is the practice management platform that puts dental practices on autopilot. When a dentist starts a new practice, instead of finding an accounting firm, hiring two front desk workers, and then spending their nights posting insurance payments, they use Denta.

I started Denta to help operate my Dad's 10 practices in Iowa. In supporting those, I realized the most painful part was both people and agents having messy access to fragmented data. So I solved for that and now spend my time automating work for dentists!

name: Markov
one_liner: Data for computer-use ai
long_description: We source high quality tasks and data to train the next generation of computer use AI models.

name: Cosmic Robotics
one_liner: Autonomous construction on Earth and beyond
long_description:
Our mission is to build a city on Mars. 

Cosmic-1, the first equipment in our fleet, is an autonomous robot capable of heavy-lift manipulation. Its been deployed on some of the largest solar farms in the US, more than doubling labor productivity across tens of thousands of panel installs. 

We're scaling in solar, expanding in data centers, and building lunar construction robotics under contract with NASA.

name: Operon
one_liner: Agentic data layer for manufacturing & process industries.
long_description:
Operon is the data intelligence layer for heavy industry. We ingest the messy documents a plant runs on (P&IDs, schematics, datasheets, scans) and turn them into one structured, queryable model of the facility.

On top of it, engineers query their plant in natural language and build agentic workflows for safety, compliance, and design.

name: Inkbox
one_liner: Give your AI agents email, phone, iMessage and an internet address
long_description: We are building the identity and communication layer for AI agents: giving agents their own email, phone, iMessage, and internet address. Every interaction flows through Inkbox, so agents are enhanced with multi-channel context.

name: Agentcard
one_liner: debit cards for AI agents.
long_description: Agentcard.sh is a debit card for AI agents. Agents can use Agentcard’s API to issue cards and pay for things online autonomously.

name: Care GP
one_liner: AI agents to run primary healthcare operations
long_description: Care GP is building AI agents to run operations for primary healthcare clinics

name: Praxis AI
one_liner: Turns every company into a data vendor
long_description: Praxis captures the data physical AI is bottlenecked on: egocentric video, 3D scans, and multimodal capture from inside real industrial and residential environments. Embedded within publicly listed and unicorn-scale conglomerates, we reach 60k workers across 5 continents and 150+ environment types, supplying the real-world training data frontier labs and humanoid companies can't source anywhere else.

name: Levocred AI
one_liner: AI for every workflow inside a credit team
long_description: Levocred AI is the Company Brain for credit teams — an AI employee that plugs into the fund's loan tape, credit agreements, servicer reports, and bank feeds, and runs the workflows analysts would otherwise grind on.

name: Mireye
one_liner: Infrastructure for Physical World AI Agents
long_description:
AI agents are starting to act in the real world, but they can't reliably understand it. 

Geospatial data is complex, fragmented, and unstructured. Ask a model a specific question about a specific place and it guesses. Our API and MCP give agents cited answers about any location.

Our mission is to index every inch of the earth, and make it as queryable as the web.

name: Hop Aero
one_liner: Rocket cargo delivery to contested environments
long_description: Hop Aero is building rocket cargo vehicles that deliver to contested environments hundreds of miles away in minutes. Our rockets fly point-to-point into the most austere locations at hypersonic speeds. Hop Aero's goal is to provide the U.S. and allies the asymmetric advantage that deters a future major conflict.

name: Florin
one_liner: The financial operating system for the companies building the future.
long_description: Florin brings banking, payments, cards, and treasury into one account built for startups - so founders can hold, move, and grow their capital without stitching together half a dozen tools. Unlike platforms that resell another bank's rails, we're building the infrastructure ourselves, end to end, so money moves in real time on a single ledger we own. We start with an invite-only membership for venture-backed companies and expand from there. Named after the florin, the coin that anchored global trade for three centuries, with infrastructure built for how money moves now.

name: Justinian
one_liner: Builders of the first AI-native government affairs firm.
long_description:
Justinian is an applied AI company changing the way companies and governments work together. 

Our first focus is building Justinian Government Affairs, an AI-native government affairs firm helping early-stage companies monitor regulations, lobby policymakers, and win government contracts.

name: Petrarch
one_liner: Market maker for specialized data assets

name: Salem Robotics Inc
one_liner: We deploy robots for inspections in hazardous spaces, like nuclear.
long_description: Our software turns any robot we deploy into an everyday independent worker at your facility. They are able to walk sites, handle instruments, overcome obstacles, collect measurements, and generate auditable compliance records, replacing hours of manual surveys and inspections. Built by Los Alamos robotics veterans and deterministic-manipulation researchers, we're starting in nuclear and expanding to other hazardous industries.

name: Coasty
one_liner: Building the future of knowledge work, RPA and SOPs using computer use
long_description:
Coasty is a computer use agent that does real work inside softwares, companies already run. New web apps, old desktop tools, the legacy systems nobody wants to touch. You hand it a workflow and it owns the outcome.
Most agents demo well and break the moment they hit real software. They get 18 steps out of 20 right, misclick once, and everything after is garbage. Coasty catches when it goes off track and fixes itself mid-task instead of starting over. It handles the messy parts, benchmarks never show. Permission dialogs, surprise pop-ups, a modal that steals focus, a page that loads slow. When a vendor pushes an overnight update and moves half the menus, brittle scripts break by morning. Coasty reads the screen instead of recording clicks, so it adapts and keeps going.
It also checks its own output and leaves a time-stamped trail you can replay. We won't pretend it gets every task right. Nothing automating this work does. What matters is what happens when something breaks. It catches the mistake, fixes what it can, and flags what it can't so a person can step in. Nothing fails quietly. That's what lets a team trust it on the work where a silent error gets someone fired.
We scored 82% on OSWorld, which puts us among the most reliable agents out there. Reliability is the whole product, not a feature.

name: Edviro
one_liner: Energy world models that fix energy waste before it becomes expensive.
long_description:
Edviro helps facilities managers at large buildings save on their energy bills. Facilities managers are overwhelmed with dashboards, alarms, and analytics for hundreds of boilers, HVAC units, and heat pumps across their buildings. Edviro continuously monitors and optimizes buildings using energy world models to detect anomalies, simulate interventions, and suggest the best options to the facilities manager.  Once the changes occur, we create a comprehensive M&V report to ensure savings show up on the bill. 

Our tools also allow institutions to maintain detailed service history & equipment logs, and perform maintenance with AR.

name: Experiential Labs
one_liner: World models for agents
long_description: An applied AI research lab building world models for agents. We simulate reality faster and more accurately based on the technology self-driving uses to get 99.7% reconstruction fidelity.

name: Simantic
one_liner: Firmware simulation for AI agents
long_description: Simantic is modernizing the embedded firmware industry with hardware simulation developed for the adoption of AI in hardware engineering. Simantic gives both humans and AI the visibility into hardware previously limited by the physical world.
